name: pax25

metadata.name: pax25
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later (AGPLv3+)

documentation:
# PAX25

pax25 is intended to be a full AX.25 stack with:

AX.25 V2 stack (not 2.2; no MOD128, no SREJ, no XID)
Multiple physical interfaces (KISS, AXIP) support

Target Python is 3.13. Earlier versions of the 3.X branch will not work, but later versions will likely work.

At present, only the application layer is working, with files as input and output. Support for real-world networking is incoming.

## Documentation

[Documentation for Pax25](https://foxyfoxie.gitlab.io/pax25/) is built and published based on the contents of the `documentation` directory. It is based on [mkdocs](https://www.mkdocs.org/), the configuration for which is found in the repository root.

modules.name: pax25.interfaces.types
documentation: Defines types used by interfaces.

classes.name: pax25.interfaces.types.Interface

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.types.Interface.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the interface. The interface will be initialized with
its name, settings, and the station it is being initialized for.

Under what conditions to set sudo is up to you, but it is set to False by
default. The sudo flag indicates whether this interface can be used for
superuser connections.

It does not automatically mean that connections on this interface will be
treated as superuser connections, but the base Application class will consider
a user a superuser if they are connected to an interface while its sudo flag is
True, and their name matches the station's default name.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.types.Interface.send_frame
documentation: Send an addressed frame through the interface.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.types.Interface.read_loop
documentation:
Interfaces should implement a read loop. The read loop is the async read
loop that pulls in from the relevent stream, be it a socket, a file, a
pipe, or whatever.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.types.Interface.shutdown
documentation:
This handles any cleanup needed to bring this interface offline, and then
allows the read_loop to end. This is usually done by setting the listening
attribute on the instance to False, so that it closes out next opportunity.

See inheriting classes for examples.

The interface base class, which defines the required functions for an interface.
All the actual functions must be defined on the subclass-- this base class
just raises NotImplementedError for all of them.

Attributes:
    listening: bool flag to indicate whether the interface is up and
        listening for packets.
    name: str name of the interface as instantiated, for internal reference.
    settings: S, a settings dictionary for the interface.
    station: Station the interface is initialized for.
    sudo: Whether this interface can be used for superuser connections.

modules.name: pax25.ax25.utils
documentation: Utility functions for working with AX.25 Frames.

functions.name: pax25.ax25.utils.should_repeat_for
documentation:
Checks if a given address is in the digipeater list and this frame requires
repeating.

functions.name: pax25.ax25.utils.repeated_for
documentation:
Returns a revised frame with the repeat flag set for the specific address.
Raises if this address isn't a repeater for the frame.

functions.name: pax25.ax25.utils.reply_digipeaters
documentation:
Returns a revised frame that has the digipeaters in reverse order and the repeated
flags all set to False.

functions.name: pax25.ax25.utils.response_frame
documentation:
Generates the base of a response frame-- source and destination are swapped,
digipeaters reversed, and contents emptied. The control is unchanged, as context
for changes to it would be kept elsewhere.

The command_or_repeated flag is swapped along with the addresses
(if it is set at all), so bear this in mind.

modules.name: pax25.interfaces.file_interface
documentation:
The file interface. This is used mostly for testing-- we can send in predefined
commands and let the system process them this way.

It's also useful for running apps over stdin/stdout, which are the default input/output
files.

documentation: pylint: disable=invalid-name

documentation:
Shorthand function for adding the first and second party to the frames we
construct.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.file_interface.FileInterface.send_frame
documentation:
Handles a frame from the frame queue.

We only really care about one frame type for this interface-- the
MessageFrame. We ignore everything else.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.file_interface.FileInterface.conditional_shutdown
documentation: If set to shut down the station automatically, go ahead and do so.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.file_interface.FileInterface.read_loop
documentation: Reads input from the keyboard and sends it for routing.

methods.name: pax25.interfaces.file_interface.FileInterface.shutdown
documentation: Closes the read loop, closes open file handles, and restores terminal.

A file interface that will read in on a file and send the resulting
bytes to another file. By default, input is stdin, and output is stdout.

You may want to set the sudo settings flag to True if this is intended to be the
administrative connection. Privileges will only be elevated if this flag is true
AND the 'source ssid' argument matches the station name, which it will by default.

modules.name: pax25.conftest
documentation: This file runs before tests are collected in order to set up the repository for tests.

modules.name: pax25.ax25.control_field
documentation:
Control Frame Types
There are three control field types in AX.25, which correspond to the three AX.25 frame
types. These are the Info, Supervisory, and Unnumbered frame types.

Semantics change significantly based on which control type is in use. Therefore, we have
multiple dataclasses for the purpose of handling these different control field types,
as well as helper functions for identifying them.

Supervisory and Informational frames come in two flavors, modulo 8 and modulo 128.
Modulo 128 uses two-byte control fields and allows for a larger sequence frame count.

For the moment, we only support modulo 8, which means we assume all control fields to be
one byte long and consider anything else to be an error. In the future, modulo 128 may
be supported.

functions.name: pax25.ax25.control_field.derive_control_class
documentation: Derives the appropriate control class for a given control byte.

modules.name: pax25.contrib
documentation:
The contrib module contains premade applications and plugins that we anticipate will be
widely useful both as examples and for practical use to client developers setting up
their own station.

These allow us to eat our own dogfood, and test the breadth of capability of the library
all in one. They are NOT the only applications we expect to develop, nor the only ones
we expect to use as examples, but they are the ones that we use for testing and as
'batteries included' functionality.

modules.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board
documentation: Bulletin Board Application Module

documentation:
Represents a route path for a frame in string form. Will add a * to the last
repeater which has repeated the frame.

Instantiate a route path from a data byte array. If there is leftover data,

methods.name: pax25.ax25.address.Ax25RoutePath.assemble

Specification for a packet's intended route of traffic-- its source,
its destination, and what digipeaters lay between.

modules.name: pax25.frame_queue
documentation:
The frame queue takes frames from the interfaces and sends them to their intended
second_party.

Some things the frame queue may handle are:

1. Creating Connection objects and attaching them to applications
2. Message forwarding
3. Beacon processing
4. Digipeating

classes.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.process_frame
documentation: Interfaces call this function to put a new frame in the queue.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.get_connection
documentation: Gets the connection, should it exist, for a frame.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.next_ssid
documentation:
Given a station name and an interface, produces the next SSID.

You can give an ssid you would prefer by specifying the ssid argument. If the
ssid already exists or is invalid, throws an exception. This function is
used primarily by the station in order to assign SSIDs to applications.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.register_station
documentation:
Registers an address as existing on a particular interface. It can then be
looked up later for making connections.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.interface_for_address
documentation:
Given a contacting interface, and an address, returns the optimal interface
for connecting to the target station. It always tries the source interface
first, followed by other known interfaces in priority order.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.connect_handler
documentation: Handles frames with connection requests.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.disconnect_handler
documentation: Handles frames with disconnection requests.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.heartbeat_handler
documentation: Handles frames with heartbeat instructions.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.beacon_handler
documentation: Handles frames with heartbeat instructions.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.message_handler
documentation: Handles frames that contain message bytes.

methods.name: pax25.frame_queue.FrameQueue.not_implemented_handler
documentation: Performs a default action when receiving an unrecognized command.

The Frame queue is a sort of router. It takes in frames from the varying interfaces
and makes sure they go to their proper destination, whether that be an existing
connection object or re-transcribed and sent out to another interface.

modules.name: pax25.applications
documentation: The applications module contains the building blocks of applications.

modules.name: pax25.applications.types
documentation: Types for applications.

classes.name: pax25.applications.types.BaseApplication

methods.name: pax25.applications.types.BaseApplication.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a copy of this application. Usually you want to store
the settings and whether this is being invoked as a proxy object.

methods.name: pax25.applications.types.BaseApplication.on_connect
documentation:
Called when a new connection is established. For instance, if we need to store
any state for the user's session, we'd set it here. You might also send a
welcome message.

methods.name: pax25.applications.types.BaseApplication.on_disconnect
documentation: Called when a connection is being disconnected. Perform any cleanup here.

methods.name: pax25.applications.types.BaseApplication.on_bytes
documentation: Called when bytes are received from a connection for this application.

methods.name: pax25.applications.types.BaseApplication.send_bytes
documentation: Call to send bytes to a particular connection.

Raw BaseApplication that implements the minimum contract that Connection and
the FrameQueue expect. In most cases you will not want to inherit from this, but
from Application instead.

classes.name: pax25.applications.types.BasicApplicationState
documentation: State for the Application.

modules.name: pax25.applications.application
documentation: Module for the Application class, used for creating all applications.

classes.name: pax25.applications.application.Application

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.setup
documentation:
Perform any initial state configuration for your application in this
function.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.is_admin
documentation: Check if the current user is an admin.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.on_connect
documentation: Called when a new connection is established.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.on_startup
documentation:
Run right after a new connection is established. You can use this function to
do any initial state configuration and/or send a welcome message.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.on_disconnect
documentation: Run when a connection is being dropped.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.on_shutdown
documentation: Called when a connection is being disconnected. Perform any cleanup here.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.on_message
documentation:
Called when a message is received. By default, this is called by
on_bytes when it detects a carriage return has been sent.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.on_bytes
documentation:
Called when bytes are received from a connection for this application. You
usually don't want to call this directly, but you might need to if you need
to control how bytes sent from the client are handled.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.send_message
documentation: Call to send a message string to a particular connection.

methods.name: pax25.applications.application.Application.send_bytes
documentation:
Call to send bytes to a particular connection. You will usually want to call
send_message instead. You might use this function if you need to control
precisely what bytes are sent to the client.

The Application class. You should inherit from this class to create your own custom
pax25 apps.

modules.name: pax25.types
documentation:
Types used for type checking the library. Client developers can use these if they like,
but they're mostly for our own sanity checks during development/testing.

functions.name: pax25.types.empty_dict_factory
documentation: Returns a cast EmptyDict to satisfy the type checker.

classes.name: pax25.types.EmptyDict
documentation: Empty dictionary.

classes.name: pax25.types.StationConfig
documentation: Configuration for a station.
The StationConfig object should always be JSON-serializable. This way we'll eventually
be able to use JSON for configuration files.
This means that all interface configs will ALSO need to be JSON-serializable.

classes.name: pax25.types.BaseFrameType
documentation: Base type for frames decoded by interfaces (and sent to them for transmission).

classes.name: pax25.types.DisconnectFrame
documentation:
Disconnect instruction frame. Used to instruct the frame queue to
perform a clean disconnection.

classes.name: pax25.types.ConnectFrame
documentation:
Connect instruction frame. Indicates the desire for one station
to connect to another on a particular address.

classes.name: pax25.types.BeaconFrame
documentation: Frame announcing the existence of a station.

classes.name: pax25.types.HeartbeatFrame
documentation:
Heartbeat instruction. Basically a no-op that verifies that everything's OK
with the connection.

classes.name: pax25.types.MessagePayload
documentation:
Payload for message frame. This only has two keys-- one, the bytes being sent.
The other is a flag for if this message is 'outbound'. In this context, outbound
means that it skips any application processing and the frame queue should send it
directly to the relevant interface.

classes.name: pax25.types.MessageFrame
documentation: Message instruction. Delivers some bytes from one address to another.

classes.name: pax25.types.ConnectionStats
documentation: Statistics produced by connections.

modules.name: pax25.connection
documentation:
Connections handle state and method for connections between two stations (or,
in some cases, one part of a station to another part of itself). They are
instantiated by the frame queue, and then handed to applications for
manipulation.

classes.name: pax25.connection.Connection

methods.name: pax25.connection.Connection.__hash__
documentation:
There should only ever be one connection between two parties on one station.
So, we can use these properties as the hash for this object, allowing us to
safely use it as a dictionary key or in a set.

methods.name: pax25.connection.Connection.interface_for
documentation: Given one of the parties, return the interface they are listening on.

methods.name: pax25.connection.Connection.close
documentation: Sends a disconnection frame.

methods.name: pax25.connection.Connection.stats
documentation:
Produces metrics about this connection.

These metrics are currently empty and not used, but when the functions
which enable them to be used are implemented, they will be populated,
and this property should be added to the documentation.

Connections handle state and configuration for a virtual circuit between
stations.

It is important to note that a connection could be loopback, which informs
some design here.

If sudo is True, signals to applications that this connection is safe to assume
as a superuser if the connected name matches the station name.

modules.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board
documentation: Main application module of the contributed bulletin board system.

functions.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.message_headers
documentation: Format the main headers of a message.

functions.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.is_party
documentation: Checks if a name (callsign) is party to a message.

functions.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.message_preview
documentation: Create a message preview string, like what would be used in message listings.

classes.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.setup
documentation: Set up the basic state of the bulletin board system.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.save_file_path
documentation: Get the path to the board's save file.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.welcome_message
documentation: Get the welcome message for the board.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.load_board_state
documentation: Load the board's state from the file path specified in the settings.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.next_id
documentation:
Get the next available board message ID. We recycle board IDs since
we could be running for a very long time, and we have space to burn.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.save_board_state
documentation:
Persist the board's state to disk. First write to a temporary file, then
overwrite the original. This way we can revert to backup if we fail mid-dump.

Don't call this directly. Await .save() instead.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.save
documentation: Locks the db for save and saves it in an awaitable manner.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.reset_state
documentation: Set the user's state to a blank default-- puts them to the home mode.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.on_startup
documentation: Set up the user's connection state and greet them.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.on_shutdown
documentation: Clear the user's connection state.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.send_prompt
documentation: Send the command prompt.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.handle_body_line
documentation: Handle input when composing a message body.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.send_listing
documentation: Given a list of messages, send all of them to the client.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.list_mine
documentation: List all messages addressed to the current user.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.can_read
documentation: Determines if a connected user has the right to read this message.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.readable
documentation: Yields all messages which can be read by a connected user.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.list_messages
documentation: List messages.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.read_mine
documentation: Read all messages addressed to the current user.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.perform_read
documentation: Send a message to a user, and mark it read.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.read_message
documentation: Read a given message ID

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.bye
documentation: Command for closing the connection.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.compose_message
documentation:
Compose a message. We'd call this function 'send' or 'send_message', but those
are reserved by the parent class.

More specifically, this starts the composition by changing the mode. The rest
of the composition work is handled in on_message.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.kill_mine
documentation: Kills all read messages addressed to the current user.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.kill_message
documentation: Delete a message from the database.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.send_info
documentation: Send an informational message about this board system.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.send_help
documentation: Send the help text.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.home_commands
documentation: Interpret the main commands available on the home screen.

methods.name: pax25.contrib.bulletin_board.bulletin_board.BulletinBoard.on_message
documentation: Perform the command routing.

A Personal Bulletin Board System (PBBS) application.

The bulletin board has the ability to persist its data to disk. By default,
it will store its data in the current working directory, but the storage file can
be customized.
